enabled.description:
Enabled defines whether the alert is enabled

Use Alert.IsEnabled() to retrieve the value of this field.

This is a pointer, because it is populated by YAML and we need to know whether it was explicitly set to a value
or not for provider.ParseWithDefaultAlert to work.

failure-threshold.description: FailureThreshold is the number of failures in a row needed before triggering the alert

success-threshold.description: SuccessThreshold defines how many successful executions must happen in a row before an ongoing incident is marked as resolved

minimum-reminder-interval.description: MinimumReminderInterval is the interval between reminders

description.description:
Description of the alert. Will be included in the alert sent.

This is a pointer, because it is populated by YAML and we need to know whether it was explicitly set to a value
or not for provider.ParseWithDefaultAlert to work.

send-on-resolved.description:
SendOnResolved defines whether to send a second notification when the issue has been resolved

This is a pointer, because it is populated by YAML and we need to know whether it was explicitly set to a value
or not for provider.ParseWithDefaultAlert to work. Use Alert.IsSendingOnResolved() for a non-pointer

provider-override.description:
ProviderOverride is an optional field that can be used to override the provider's configuration
It is freeform so that it can be used for any provider-specific configuration.

default-alert.description: DefaultAlert is the default alert configuration to use for endpoints with an alert of the appropriate type

overrides.description: Overrides is a list of Override that may be prioritized over the default configuration

timestamp.description: Timestamp is the UTC timestamp when the announcement was made

type.description: Type is the type of announcement (outage, warning, information, operational, none)

message.description: Message is the user-facing text describing the announcement

archived.description:
Archived indicates whether the announcement should be displayed in the historical section
instead of at the top of the status page

username.description: Username is the name which will need to be used for a successful authentication

password-bcrypt-base64.description:
PasswordBcryptHashBase64Encoded is the base64 encoded string of the Bcrypt hash of the password to use to
authenticate using basic auth.

BasicConfig.description: BasicConfig is the configuration for Basic authentication

Checker.description: Checker is the configuration for making sure Gatus has access to the internet.

default-alert.description: DefaultAlert is the default alert configuration to use for endpoints with an alert of the appropriate type

overrides.description: Overrides is a list of Override that may be prioritized over the default configuration

ClickupAlertProvider.description: AlertProvider is the configuration necessary for sending an alert using ClickUp

ClickupOverride.description: Override is a case under which the default configuration is overridden

proxy-url.description: ProxyURL is the URL of the proxy to use for the client

insecure.description: Insecure determines whether to skip verifying the server's certificate chain and host name

ignore-redirect.description: IgnoreRedirect determines whether to ignore redirects (true) or follow them (false, default)

dns-resolver.description:
DNSResolver override for the HTTP client
Expected format is {protocol}://{host}:{port}, e.g. tcp://8.8.8.8:53

oauth2.description:
OAuth2Config is the OAuth2 configuration used for the client.

If non-nil, the http.Client returned by getHTTPClient will automatically retrieve a token if necessary.
See configureOAuth2 for more details.

identity-aware-proxy.description: IAPConfig is the Google Cloud Identity-Aware-Proxy configuration used for the client. (e.g. audience)

network.description: Network (ip, ip4 or ip6) for the ICMP client

tunnel.description: Tunnel is the name of the SSH tunnel to use for the client

ClientConfig.description: Config is the configuration for clients

debug.description:
Debug Whether to enable debug logs
Deprecated: Use the GATUS_LOG_LEVEL environment variable instead

metrics.description: Metrics Whether to expose metrics at /metrics

skip-invalid-config-update.description:
SkipInvalidConfigUpdate Whether to make the application ignore invalid configuration
if the configuration file is updated while the application is running

disable-monitoring-lock.description:
DisableMonitoringLock Whether to disable the monitoring lock
The monitoring lock is what prevents multiple endpoints from being processed at the same time.
Disabling this may lead to inaccurate response times

Deprecated: Use Concurrency instead TODO: REMOVE THIS IN v6.0.0

concurrency.description:
Concurrency is the maximum number of endpoints/suites that can be monitored concurrently
Defaults to DefaultConcurrency. Set to 0 for unlimited concurrency.

security.description: Security is the configuration for securing access to Gatus

alerting.description: Alerting is the configuration for alerting providers

endpoints.description: Endpoints is the list of endpoints to monitor

external-endpoints.description: ExternalEndpoints is the list of all external endpoints

suites.description: Suites is the list of suites to monitor

storage.description: Storage is the configuration for how the data is stored

web.description: Web is the web configuration for the application

ui.description: UI is the configuration for the UI

maintenance.description: Maintenance is the configuration for creating a maintenance window in which no alerts are sent

remote.description:
Remote is the configuration for remote Gatus instances
WARNING: This is in ALPHA and may change or be completely removed in the future

connectivity.description: Connectivity is the configuration for connectivity

tunneling.description: Tunneling is the configuration for SSH tunneling

announcements.description: Announcements is the list of system-wide announcements

enabled.description: Enabled defines whether to enable the monitoring of the endpoint

name.description: Name of the endpoint. Can be anything.

group.description: Group the endpoint is a part of. Used for grouping multiple endpoints together on the front end.

url.description: URL to send the request to

method.description: Method of the request made to the url of the endpoint

graphql.description: GraphQL is whether to wrap the body in a query param ({"query":"$body"})

extra-labels.description: ExtraLabels are key-value pairs that can be used to metric the endpoint

interval.description: Interval is the duration to wait between every status check

conditions.description: Conditions used to determine the health of the endpoint

alerts.description: Alerts is the alerting configuration for the endpoint in case of failure

maintenance-windows.description: MaintenanceWindow is the configuration for per-endpoint maintenance windows

dns.description: DNSConfig is the configuration for DNS monitoring

ssh.description: SSH is the configuration for SSH monitoring

client.description: ClientConfig is the configuration of the client used to communicate with the endpoint's target

ui.description: UIConfig is the configuration for the UI

store.description:
Store is a map of values to extract from the result and store in the suite context
This field is only used when the endpoint is part of a suite

always-run.description:
AlwaysRun defines whether to execute this endpoint even if previous endpoints in the suite failed
This field is only used when the endpoint is part of a suite

Endpoint.description: Endpoint is the configuration of a service to be monitored

enabled.description: Enabled defines whether to enable the monitoring of the endpoint

name.description: Name of the endpoint. Can be anything.

group.description: Group the endpoint is a part of. Used for grouping multiple endpoints together on the front end.

token.description: Token is the bearer token that must be provided through the Authorization header to push results to the endpoint

alerts.description: Alerts is the alerting configuration for the endpoint in case of failure

maintenance-windows.description: MaintenanceWindow is the configuration for per-endpoint maintenance windows

heartbeat.description: Heartbeat is the configuration that checks if the external endpoint has received new results when it should have.

ExternalEndpoint.description: ExternalEndpoint is an endpoint whose result is pushed from outside Gatus, which means that said endpoints are not monitored by Gatus itself; Gatus only displays their results and takes care of alerting

application-token.description:
Key used to authenticate the application sending
See "Your Applications" on the dashboard, or add a new one: https://pushover.net/apps/build

user-key.description: Key of the user or group the messages should be sent to

title.description:
The title of your message
default: "Gatus: <endpoint>""

priority.description:
Priority of all messages, ranging from -2 (very low) to 2 (Emergency)
default: 0

resolved-priority.description:
Priority of resolved messages, ranging from -2 (very low) to 2 (Emergency)
default: 0

sound.description:
Sound of the messages (see: https://pushover.net/api#sounds)
default: "" (pushover)

ttl.description:
TTL of your message (https://pushover.net/api#ttl)
If priority is 2 then this parameter is ignored
default: 0

device.description:
Device to send the message to (see: https://pushover.net/api#devices)
default: "" (all devices)

path.description:
Path is the path used by the store to achieve persistence
If blank, persistence is disabled.
Note that not all Type support persistence

type.description:
Type of store
If blank, uses the default in-memory store

caching.description:
Caching is whether to enable caching.
This is used to drastically decrease read latency by pre-emptively caching writes
as they happen, also known as the write-through caching strategy.
Does not apply if Config.Type is not TypePostgres or TypeSQLite.

maximum-number-of-results.description: MaximumNumberOfResults is the number of results each endpoint should be able to provide

maximum-number-of-events.description: MaximumNumberOfEvents is the number of events each endpoint should be able to provide

group.description: Group the suite belongs to. Used for grouping multiple suites together.

enabled.description: Enabled defines whether the suite is enabled

interval.description: Interval is the duration to wait between suite executions

timeout.description: Timeout is the maximum duration for the entire suite execution

context.description: InitialContext holds initial values that can be referenced by endpoints

Suite.description: Suite is a collection of endpoints that are executed sequentially with shared context

certificate-file.description: CertificateFile is the public certificate for TLS in PEM format.

hide-conditions.description: HideConditions whether to hide the condition results on the UI

hide-hostname.description: HideHostname whether to hide the hostname in the Result

hide-url.description: HideURL whether to ensure the URL is not displayed in the results. Useful if the URL contains a token.

hide-port.description: HidePort whether to hide the port in the Result

hide-errors.description: HideErrors whether to hide the errors in the Result

dont-resolve-failed-conditions.description: DontResolveFailedConditions whether to resolve failed conditions in the Result for display in the UI

resolve-successful-conditions.description: ResolveSuccessfulConditions whether to resolve successful conditions in the Result for display in the UI

badge.description: Badge is the configuration for the badges generated

UiConfig.description: Config is the UI configuration for endpoint.Endpoint

default-alert.description: DefaultAlert is the default alert configuration to use for endpoints with an alert of the appropriate type

overrides.description: Overrides is a list of Override that may be prioritized over the default configuration

VonageAlertProvider.description: AlertProvider is the configuration necessary for sending an alert using Vonage

VonageOverride.description: Override is a case under which the default integration is overridden

address.description: Address to listen on (defaults to 0.0.0.0 specified by DefaultAddress)

port.description: Port to listen on (default to 8080 specified by DefaultPort)

read-buffer-size.description:
ReadBufferSize sets fiber.Config.ReadBufferSize, which is the buffer size for reading requests coming from a
single connection and also acts as a limit for the maximum header size.

If you're getting occasional "Request Header Fields Too Large", you may want to try increasing this value.

Defaults to DefaultReadBufferSize

WebConfig.description: Config is the structure which supports the configuration of the server listening to requests

certificate-file.description: CertificateFile is the public certificate for TLS in PEM format.

private-key-file.description: PrivateKeyFile is the private key file for TLS in PEM format.

default-alert.description: DefaultAlert is the default alert configuration to use for endpoints with an alert of the appropriate type

overrides.description: Overrides is a list of Override that may be prioritized over the default configuration

WebexAlertProvider.description: AlertProvider is the configuration necessary for sending an alert using Webex

WebexOverride.description: Override is a case under which the default integration is overridden

default-alert.description: DefaultAlert is the default alert configuration to use for endpoints with an alert of the appropriate type

overrides.description: Overrides is a list of Override that may be prioritized over the default configuration

ZapierAlertProvider.description: AlertProvider is the configuration necessary for sending an alert using Zapier

ZapierOverride.description: Override is a case under which the default integration is overridden

bot-api-key.description: API key of the bot user

channel-id.description: ID of the channel to send the message to

overrides.description: Overrides is a list of Override that may be prioritized over the default configuration
